task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for ThisSP/Authenticate; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists empty ACL for that row, and the target SUCCESS returned exactly that list.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. "", "AddACEACL": "", "RemoveACEACL": "", "GetACLACL": "ACE_Anybody", "DeleteMethodACL": "", "ACLAddACELog": "", "RemoveACELog": "", "GetACLLog": "", "DeleteMethodLog": "", "LogTo": "" }, { "UID": "", evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for ThisSP/RevertSP; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists empty ACL for that row, and the target SUCCESS returned exactly that list.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for SPInfo/Get; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists empty ACL for that row, and the target SUCCESS returned exactly that list.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. case_name: Locking SP SPInfo GetACL returns empty ACL despite CommonName Anybody: impossible return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for 0000000100000000/Get; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists ACE_Anybody for that row, and the target SUCCESS returned exactly that list.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. label: PASS
rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for 0000000300000001/SPTemplatesObj; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists ACE_Anybody for that row, and the target SUCCESS returned exactly that list.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for 0000000600000008/MethodIDObj; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists ACE_Anybody for that row, and the target SUCCESS returned exactly that list.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.
label: PASS
rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for ACETable/Next; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists ACE_Anybody for that row, and the target SUCCESS returned exactly that list.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

label: PASS
rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for 000000080003A801/Set; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression for that row, and the target SUCCESS returned exactly that list.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for Authority_User1/Get; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists ACE_Authority_Get_All, ACE_Anybody_Get_CommonName for that row, but the target SUCCESS returned ACE_Authority_Set_Enabled, ACE_Anybody_Get_CommonName.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. rationale: GetACL retrieves the AccessControl ACL column for 0000000900010001/Set; the Locking SP preconfiguration lists ACE_Admins_Set_CommonName for that row, but the target SUCCESS returned ACE_Authority_Set_Enabled, ACE_Admins_Set_CommonName.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves the ACL column for an AccessControl association and returns the access_control_list as ACE uidrefs. In the Opal Locking SP AccessControl preconfiguration, CommonName is not the ACL column: rows such as ThisSP Authenticate/Random/RevertSP and SPInfo Get have CommonName ACE_Anybody but an empty ACL, so successful GetACL returns an empty ACE list. The same table also preconfigures exact non-empty ACLs for ACE object Get/Set, Authority Get/Set, C_PIN Get/Set, SecretProtect, LockingInfo, MBR, and table/object rows; a successful GetACL response must return exactly the ACE uidrefs listed in the ACL column.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.3.1.6 AccessControl (M) Table 38 contains Optional rows designated with (O). Start of Informative Comment *AC1: refer to section 5.1.2.3 for details on the requirements for supporting RevertSP *AC8: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SecretProtect object UIDs End of Informative Comment *AC2: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the Table object UIDs *AC3: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the SPTemplates object UIDs *AC4: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the MethodID object UIDs *AC5: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSBs of the ACE object UIDs *AC6: only K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 related rows are Mandatory *AC7: the notation of “TT TT TT TT” represents a shorthand for the LSB of the Authority object UIDs Notes: • The AccessControl table is different from any other table defined in this specification. Although cells in this table are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). • The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method.
